title: Andrew Rose
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Andrew Rose
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
education_entries: Ph D in English Literature and Language, University of Washington
MA in English Literature and Language, University of Washington
BA in English Literature, Lafayette College
research_interests: Environmental Rhetoric, Environment and Literature (ecocriticism), Posthumanism, Environmental Justice and Climate Change Environmentalism
teaching_interests: Environmental Rhetoric and Communication, Science Writing, Environmental Literature, American Literature
publications: Journal Article, Academic Journal (2024). The Politics of Habitability in Thoreau's 'Walking' . The Concord Saunterer: A Journal of Thoreau Studies / The Thoreau Society. Volume, 32. Issue, 2023.
Monograph (2021). Material Insurgency: Towards a Distributed Environmental Politics . State University of New York (SUNY) Press.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2019). Insurgency and Distributed Agency in Karen Tei Yamashita's "Through the Arc of the Rainforest" . Interdisciplinary Studies in Literature and the Environment/Oxford Press. Volume, 26. Issue, 1. Pages, 125-144.

Journal Article, Academic Journal (2016). The Unknowable Now: Passionate Science and Transformative Politics in Kim Stanley Robinson's 'Science in the Capital' trilogy . Science Fiction Studies. Volume, 43.2. Issue, 129. Pages, 260-286.
